---
|
---
| **County grants $1 million to prep Cow Palace for disasters**
---
| Dear friends,

The San Mateo County Board of Supervisors approved a $4.4 billion revised budget for fiscal year 2023-24 Sept. 26 that includes $1 million for renovations to the Cow Palace in Daly City. The grant that I championed will now allow the iconic arena to be used as an evacuation, care and shelter site for county residents and livestock when disaster strikes.

The Cow Palace has proven invaluable during emergencies, especially during the CZU wildfire when it became a Noah’s Ark for county residents scrambling to find shelter for horses, cows, goats and even llamas. With this $1 million, the Cow Palace will now be equipped to serve thousands of meals a day and keep the lights on with new emergency generators during power outages. This is another investment in emergency preparedness that highlights the county’s continuing commitment to public safety.

The $1 million investment in the Cow Palace supports substantial emergency preparedness renovations. Specifically, the Cow Palace will use the funds toward (1) remodeling the kitchen, freezer, and dining hall to facilitate the provision of thousands of meals per day;
---
(2) installing additional permanent emergency generators to sustain power during outages; (3) acquiring and appropriately storing care supplies including blankets, cots, and comfort kits; and (4) repairing and renovating large animal/livestock evacuation and care areas.

For the investment, San Mateo County was the recipient of the Western Fairs Association Blue Ribbon Award for exemplary service and support, which was presented to myself presented in the arena during Saturday opening night of the 75th annual Grand National Rodeo, one of the most iconic annual events in all of California that showcases why this arena is truly the Cow Palace. This family-friendly rodeo harkens back to our state’s rich agricultural history.

The budget also prioritizes funds designated for farm labor housing, affordable housing, expanding fire mitigation measures and increasing funding for crisis response teams, Mental Health Services Act and reaching functional zero in homelessness.
---
| **Midway Early Learning Center a testament to collaboration** I was honored to celebrate San Mateo County’s $1 million contribution I helped secure to renovate the Midway Early Learning Center in Daly City’s Bayshore neighborhood. This investment helps address the lack of childcare centers in the county, particularly for low-income working families. The learning center is operated by the nonprofit Peninsula Family Service which plays a vital role in our community by providing support, resources and compassion to children, families and older adults.

I attended the Celebrating You event organized by Peninsula Family Service Thursday, Oct. 19 at the beautiful Farallon Room at my alma mater Skyline College. This event was a tribute to the generous donors of Peninsula Family Service and it was a privilege to be part of the Circle of Midway Champions celebrating the incredible efforts that led to the complete transformation of the Midway Early Learning Center.

We’ve collectively authored a story filled with hope and possibility for the most vulnerable members of our community, equipping them with the tools they need to thrive. I want to extend my heartfelt gratitude to Heather Cleary, PFS CEO, Debbie Harrison, Elizabeth Jenson, Midway Co-Chairs, Ron Lynch, PFS Governing Board Chair, and the Hillsborough Auxiliary for their incredible dedication to strengthening our community.
---
---
| **
Board announces top priorities for Measure K** The Board of Supervisors has announced its top priorities for Measure K dollars after months of community outreach. We’ve heard loud and clear that our residents want to see more investments in children, families and seniors, emergency preparedness and that affordable housing and ending homelessness should be the focus of how the county spends this Measure K money through the many listening sessions conducted in all five districts and through the input of thousands of survey takers. Investments in mental health will also be made across all three priority areas.

Our older adult population also made it clear that they should have their own category as they often feel that they are overlooked or left in the shadows. These priority areas come after months of subcommittee meetings with Supervisor Pine, myself and the county executive’s office.

Approximately $43 million is unallocated for these priorities out of $110 million the county expects the Measure K 1⁄2 cent sales tax to generate this fiscal year.
As a Member of the Measure K subcommittee, I have worked hard the past few months to prioritize: Childcare centers and in-home childcare; career development in childcare; domestic violence prevention; language accessibility; and housing assistance for older adults in need.

These priorities guide grant allocations, with applications opening in November and closing in December for community-based organizations that partner with the county to provide these critical services. Award announcements will be made between March and June.
---
| **
Colma granted $120,000 for EV charging stations** To empower the journey to sustainability in District 5, I've allocated the town of Colma $120,000 in Measure K funding to enhance the electric vehicle charging infrastructure for the Colma Community Center EV Charging Station Project.

This crucial investment not only fosters sustainability but also contributes to reduced greenhouse gas emissions in the San Mateo County region. Aligning with the town’s 2023-2025 Strategic Plan, we're prioritizing green initiatives and making electric charging stations accessible for our growing EV community.

With more and more of our residents buying electric vehicles, it is critical to continue to add EV charging stations to our infrastructure. Gas-guzzling vehicles are the biggest producers of greenhouse gas emissions in the Bay Area and a leading contributor to climate change. Let’s save the planet by going electric!
---
| **Battling food insecurity **
Food insecurity is a problem some residents may still face as we continue to emerge from the pandemic. Here are some resources residents can access: **CalFresh:**
Is a program for people with low-income who meet federal income eligibility rules and want to add to their budget to put healthy and nutritious food on the table. Website: [https://www.cdss.ca.gov/food-nutrition/calfresh](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=e52f33712e&e=c0babbc643) Contact: (650) 802-6576, [antorres@smcgov.org](mailto:antorres@smcgov.org) **Daly City Partnership:**
DC Partnership is a local partnership that was formed to guide collaborative efforts and connect our community to services that promote well-being. Check out their website for times and locations. Website: [https://www.dcpartnership.org/covid19/](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=d96e85570e&e=c0babbc643) **Second Harvest Food Bank:**
Second Harvest offers food at various locations throughout San Mateo County listed on its webpage. Website: [https://www.shfb.org/get-food/](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=953ae69ab5&e=c0babbc643) Contacts: Call (800) 984-3663 or text GETFOOD to (408) 455-5181 **Interns wanted to join our team **

We are looking to add to our great group of interns we are currently working with. If you or someone you know may be interested in applying to my Internship Program, submitting this form is the first step: [https://forms.gle/hiKqtn7Rird5vXwB9](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=abc1d523a5&e=c0babbc643). You can also reach out to Mike Richardson on my staff directly with any questions at [mrichardson@smcgov.org](mailto:mrichardson@smcgov.org) In friendship,
David J. Canepa
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| | |
---
| _Copyright © 2023 Supervisor David Canepa, All rights reserved._
You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

subject: Our fight for LGBTQ justice
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
We need leaders like Pramila who are ready to stand up to these extremists and denounce their draconian legislation before more harm is done.
#
Peter,
Florida Governor and GOP presidential candidate Ron DeSantis is an anti-trans bigot who peddles dangerous rhetoric that targets marginalized communities.
**He has no business being anywhere near the Oval Office.**
[**If you stand with Pramila as she fights to end bigotry and defend LGBTQ rights, please chip in to power our progress.**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/46548?refcode=em20231023-2-3075&t=1&akid=3075%2E420207%2E50B6bX)
**[Donate now ››](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/46548?refcode=em20231023-2-3075&t=20&akid=3075%2E420207%2E50B6bX)**
**Ron DeSantis has relentlessly targeted the LGBTQ community during his time in office.** He demonizes gender-affirming care for minors, drag shows, and any mention of gender identity in schools.
He does NOT represent what our country values.
We need leaders like Pramila who are ready to stand up to these extremists and denounce their draconian legislation before more harm is done.**[Donate today to strengthen our fight for LGBTQ justice. ](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/46548?refcode=em20231023-2-3075&t=21&akid=3075%2E420207%2E50B6bX)**
Together,
Team Pramila

Peter, this is horrible:
**Last month, House Republicans introduced a bill** **to BAN medication abortion**** ****– the most common method of abortion in America.**
Make no mistake: This abortion pill, mifepristone, has been FDA-approved and safely used for _23 years_. Banning this pill is just their latest effort to stop abortion nationwide.
We will not stand idly by as Republicans keep working to take away our fundamental right to make our own reproductive health care decisions. We hope you won't either, Peter.
**We need every member of this team to speak out, but you're running out of time to answer our poll before it closes at midnight tonight. Please, lock in your response now:**

body:
| | | [](http://links.msgop.co/u/click?_t=8490f3f542f84240a99e9ceb9c894519&_m=61898d010d4141afa44f2186ee77a9ca&_e=76s2DPG_BjpksmFWsGEWD7EUWlnFag6TAZMKD6AgdmTCm0lbc_M5S_KTInrz_UG98ROBrKivvGLqCp5qnMcFzeBD_9YVZp1vxyKxTDw2t25vFWTI4J8oPcEzuztw0WOOX8OK8VPz0P2q5GYMMUT4_efzieXZvpfrEinZmdrqyaLVZWoXo3Gf9YA0-a8DL8T6Zm8h85vZd164oV-hUWRkjiO4EREjeGTmyQuAFPNTCfD-fx31MKzg6p08n3k5FFEUVrQ6Eaoc5c8qfsw31PLwcRsaXoqdSFRK4Ff3vZyYWXlBObl7a8E0Bkvz-YN60Hdkv_4mfUWlTykddPQVQy4j2w%3D%3D)
---
Fellow conservative, in just a few short weeks, you’ll decide what future you want for Mississippi.

You’ll decide the future you want for your kids, your career, your retirement, and the future of those for generations to come.

Our country and our state are at a pivotal point. This election will shape Mississippi for DECADES to come and we need to make sure those decades are PROSPEROUS.

**There is one way to do that and that is by voting conservative.**

We know patriots like you will always fight for conservative values but there are A LOT of undecided voters who we have to reach if we want any chance of winning.

**[Will you pitch in today to make sure we can find and reach these voters before its too late?](http://links.msgop.co/u/click?_t=8490f3f542f84240a99e9ceb9c894519&_m=61898d010d4141afa44f2186ee77a9ca&_e=76s2DPG_BjpksmFWsGEWD7EUWlnFag6TAZMKD6AgdmTCm0lbc_M5S_KTInrz_UG98ROBrKivvGLqCp5qnMcFzeBD_9YVZp1vxyKxTDw2t25vFWTI4J8oPcEzuztw0WOOX8OK8VPz0P2q5GYMMUT4_efzieXZvpfrEinZmdrqyaLVZWoXo3Gf9YA0-a8DL8T6YULL9TTjOLWxmm9m3N3truFZgwqzI45srr2PuNET7rg6h9S9OOpRi8UCxc3MpfWIlh7g1RdTn4CmIWbjHDYlccGYMhIS3a1kihzt04dG3Z8-hVslU7UCkpHCMC5wa7B0ZiAOBui3mcrJLyYy5aHvhA%3D%3D)**

clean_body: | | | Fellow conservative, in just a few short weeks, you’ll decide what future you want for Mississippi. You’ll decide the future you want for your kids, your career, your retirement, and the future of those for generations to come. Our country and our state are at a pivotal point. This election will shape Mississippi for DECADES to come and we need to make sure those decades are PROSPEROUS. There is one way to do that and that is by voting conservative. We know patriots like you will always fight for conservative values but there are A LOT of undecided voters who we have to reach if we want any chance of winning. Thank You, Mississippi Conservatives | | Paid for by the Mississippi Republican Party | |
